heading: Reporting on demarches
label: 6304 Reporting on demarches
notes: Effective DateSection effective 60 days after , see , set out as a note under .Apr. 30, 1994section 831 of Pub. L. 103–236section 6301 of this title

children.text: It is the sense of the Congress that the Department of State should, in the course of implementing its reporting responsibilities under , include a summary of demarches that the United States has issued or received from foreign governments with respect to activities which are of significance from the proliferation standpoint.section 3282(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a concern over a past, present, or possible future action or activity of the recipient government, or of a person within the jurisdiction of that government, contributing to the global spread of unsafeguarded special nuclear material or of nuclear explosive devices;

children.label: B
children.text: a request for the recipient government to counter such action or activity; or

children.label: C
children.text: both the concern and request described in subparagraphs (A) and (B).
